ProjectTitle: 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition - RFB 2023-04
Summary: 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition - RFB 2023-04. Action: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2023-04, 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition to Southwest Builders Inc. to demolish the structure located at 8801 Corkscrew Road at a grand total cost of $106,890, approve a contingency fund amount of $10,689 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract documents on beh...
ActionTaken: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2023-04, 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition to Southwest Builders Inc. to demolish the structure located at 8801 Corkscrew Road at a grand total cost of $106,890, approve a contingency fund amount of $10,689 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract documents on behalf of the Village of Estero Council with the instructions that the Village Manager is not to move forward with the demolition until further direction from Council. Additionally, Resolution No. 2023-02 was clarified to correct the date of execution from March 15, 2023, to February 15, 2023 due to error
Outcome: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2023-04, 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition to Southwest Builders Inc. to demolish the structure located at 8801 Corkscrew Road at a grand total cost of $106,890, approve a contingency fund amount of $10,689 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract documents on behalf of the Village of Estero Council with the instructions that the Village Manager is not to move forward with the demolition until further direction from Council. Additionally, Resolution No. 2023-02 was clarified to correct the date of execution from March 15, 2023, to February 15, 2023 due to error
MotionText: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2023-04, 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition to Southwest Builders Inc. to demolish the structure located at 8801 Corkscrew Road at a grand total cost of $106,890, approve a contingency fund amount of $10,689 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract documents on behalf of the Village of Estero Council with the instructions that the Village Manager is not to move forward with the demolition until further direction from Council. Additionally, Resolution No. 2023-02 was clarified to correct the date of execution from March 15, 2023, to February 15, 2023 due to error

ActionTaken: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2023-04, 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition to Southwest Builders Inc. to demolish the structure located at 8801 Corkscrew Road at a grand total cost of $106,890, approve a contingency fund amount of $10,689 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract documents on behalf of the Village of Estero Council with the instructions that the Village Manager is not to move forward with the demolition until further direction from Council. Additionally, Resolution No. 2023-02 was clarified to correct the date of execution from March 15, 2023, to February 15, 2023 due to error
Outcome: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2023-04, 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition to Southwest Builders Inc. to demolish the structure located at 8801 Corkscrew Road at a grand total cost of $106,890, approve a contingency fund amount of $10,689 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract documents on behalf of the Village of Estero Council with the instructions that the Village Manager is not to move forward with the demolition until further direction from Council. Additionally, Resolution No. 2023-02 was clarified to correct the date of execution from March 15, 2023, to February 15, 2023 due to error
MotionText: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2023-04, 8801 Corkscrew Road Structure Demolition to Southwest Builders Inc. to demolish the structure located at 8801 Corkscrew Road at a grand total cost of $106,890, approve a contingency fund amount of $10,689 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur, and authorize the Village Manager to execute the contract documents on behalf of the Village of Estero Council with the instructions that the Village Manager is not to move forward with the demolition until further direction from Council. Additionally, Resolution No. 2023-02 was clarified to correct the date of execution from March 15, 2023, to February 15, 2023 due to error

ProjectTitle: Village Wide Traffic Study Contract
Summary: Village Wide Traffic Study Contract. Action: Approved Contract EC 2024-07 with Johnson Engineering, Inc. to prepare the Village Wide Traffic Study for $249,480, approve a $25,000 contingency for additional services that may be required to complete the project, authorize the Village Manager to sign the contract and other additional implementing documents within the scope of the contract on behalf of the Village of Estero Council, and authorize the Village Manager to execute an amendment to the...
ActionTaken: Approved Contract EC 2024-07 with Johnson Engineering, Inc. to prepare the Village Wide Traffic Study for $249,480, approve a $25,000 contingency for additional services that may be required to complete the project, authorize the Village Manager to sign the contract and other additional implementing documents within the scope of the contract on behalf of the Village of Estero Council, and authorize the Village Manager to execute an amendment to the agreement to add the locations added by council. Vice Mayor Ribble would like to add the intersection Meadowbrook and Coconut to the study. Councilmember Zalucki would like to add the intersections Corkscrew and Estero Crossing Blvd and Corkscrew and Bella Terra Blvd to the study
Outcome: Approved Contract EC 2024-07 with Johnson Engineering, Inc. to prepare the Village Wide Traffic Study for $249,480, approve a $25,000 contingency for additional services that may be required to complete the project, authorize the Village Manager to sign the contract and other additional implementing documents within the scope of the contract on behalf of the Village of Estero Council, and authorize the Village Manager to execute an amendment to the agreement to add the locations added by council. Vice Mayor Ribble would like to add the intersection Meadowbrook and Coconut to the study. Councilmember Zalucki would like to add the intersections Corkscrew and Estero Crossing Blvd and Corkscrew and Bella Terra Blvd to the study
MotionText: Approved Contract EC 2024-07 with Johnson Engineering, Inc. to prepare the Village Wide Traffic Study for $249,480, approve a $25,000 contingency for additional services that may be required to complete the project, authorize the Village Manager to sign the contract and other additional implementing documents within the scope of the contract on behalf of the Village of Estero Council, and authorize the Village Manager to execute an amendment to the agreement to add the locations added by council. Vice Mayor Ribble would like to add the intersection Meadowbrook and Coconut to the study. Councilmember Zalucki would like to add the intersections Corkscrew and Estero Crossing Blvd and Corkscrew and Bella Terra Blvd to the study

He continued that he was not in favor of fracking; however, he wanted to be practical and put in laws that meant something. ActionTaken: adopted prior to December 31, 2014; however, it is an exemption that the Village will get next year. Attorney Saunders added that it only applied to the very small millage that the Village of Estero would be levying. Discussion followed regarding the net effect on tax payers. Vice Mayor Levitan inquired whether a resolution would need to be adopted every year. Village Manager Peter Lombardi responded that the Village would need to adopt an ordinance because the County ordinance excludes the incorporated area of Estero; the Village ordinance would put the provision into effect; once the ordinance is adopted, it remains until it is rescinded. Attorney Saunders stated that he would probably have an ordinance on the agenda in September. ActionTaken: Approved Option 2: Approved award of Request for Bids No. RFB 2019-06; Estero Parkway Roadway and Landscape Improvements to Cougar Contracting LLC at a Grand Total Cost of $7,262,000. Excluding the bid alternate for roadway lighting. Approved a contingency fund amount of $727,000 (an amount equal to 10% of the total project cost) to cover unforeseen circumstances which may occur. The budget for Estero Parkway is $9,300,000 which would fully fund the total project costs of $8,973,000. Authorized the Village Manager to execute the contract and any other related ancillary documents on behalf of the Village ofEstero Council. Additionally, within 90 days, have staff come back to Council with information regarding value engineering and direct staff to identify potential streetlight standards and present potential alternatives and costs, including maintenance costs and information related to solar powered lights

Attorney Saunders added that it only applied to the very small millage that the Village of Estero would be levying. Discussion followed regarding the net effect on tax payers. Vice Mayor Levitan inquired whether a resolution would need to be adopted every year. Village Manager Peter Lombardi responded that the Village would need to adopt an ordinance because the County ordinance excludes the incorporated area of Estero; the Village ordinance would put the provision into effect; once the ordinance is adopted, it remains until it is rescinded. Summary: approved by the ECCL members present at the August 28, 2015 meeting related to including a list of civic, recreational and arts, and entertainment assets in the section of the Village Center south of Corkscrew Road and. expanded public, bike, and pedestrian access to the Estero River, improved integration of the Koreshan State Site and the Boomer/future State park property with the historic and river access properties in the Old Estero area north of the Estero River into an expanded Village C...

ActionTaken: adopted prior to December 31, 2014; however, it is an exemption that the Village will get next year. Attorney Saunders added that it only applied to the very small millage that the Village of Estero would be levying. Discussion followed regarding the net effect on tax payers. Vice Mayor Levitan inquired whether a resolution would need to be adopted every year. Village Manager Peter Lombardi responded that the Village would need to adopt an ordinance because the County ordinance excludes the incorporated area of Estero; the Village ordinance would put the provision into effect; once the ordinance is adopted, it remains until it is rescinded. Attorney Saunders stated that he would probably have an ordinance on the agenda in September. Brief discussion followed regarding County ordinances that differentiate between unincorporated areas and municipal areas and an opinion that was received from the County Attorney's office that they would still be collecting impact fees even though Ester...

Summary: Continued first reading of Ordinance No. 2019-27 and referred it to the Design Review Board to review the concems/issues that Councilmembers raised related to: the lack of details in the Pattern Book; landscaping buffer (berm) details on the north side of the property; additional detail on landscaping along Corkscrew Road and Three Oaks Parkway; the failing condition of the roadway in front of the property; utilities shielding; information related to the opening date of the business and how i...
ActionTaken: Continued first reading of Ordinance No. 2019-27 and referred it to the Design Review Board to review the concems/issues that Councilmembers raised related to: the lack of details in the Pattern Book; landscaping buffer (berm) details on the north side of the property; additional detail on landscaping along Corkscrew Road and Three Oaks Parkway; the failing condition of the roadway in front of the property; utilities shielding; information related to the opening date of the business and how it coincides with the interchange improvements; reduction of parking spaces and possibility of increasing the number of handicap spaces; vehicle stacking; improvement to the building architecture, specifically the flat surfaces; minimal amount of outdoor seats; and the site plan
Outcome: Continued first reading of Ordinance No. 2019-27 and referred it to the Design Review Board to review the concems/issues that Councilmembers raised related to: the lack of details in the Pattern Book; landscaping buffer (berm) details on the north side of the property; additional detail on landscaping along Corkscrew Road and Three Oaks Parkway; the failing condition of the roadway in front of the property; utilities shielding; information related to the opening date of the business and how it coincides with the interchange improvements; reduction of parking spaces and possibility of increasing the number of handicap spaces; vehicle stacking; improvement to the building architecture, specifically the flat surfaces; minimal amount of outdoor seats; and the site plan
MotionText: Continued first reading of Ordinance No. 2019-27 and referred it to the Design Review Board to review the concems/issues that Councilmembers raised related to: the lack of details in the Pattern Book; landscaping buffer (berm) details on the north side of the property; additional detail on landscaping along Corkscrew Road and Three Oaks Parkway; the failing condition of the roadway in front of the property; utilities shielding; information related to the opening date of the business and how it coincides with the interchange improvements; reduction of parking spaces and possibility of increasing the number of handicap spaces; vehicle stacking; improvement to the building architecture, specifically the flat surfaces; minimal amount of outdoor seats; and the site plan

ActionTaken: approved in Item 5(G); and that a revised Map "B" had been circulated on April 14, 2015. Following brief Council discussion as to whether it would be appropriate to include the revised map in today's action, Special Zoning Counsel Nancy Stroud provided a detailed explanation of the purpose, requirements and limitations of "Zoning in Progress". She also addressed concerns and misconceptions contained in letters from residents, developers and landowners. Vice-Mayor Levitan offered comments regarding the concept of mixed use centers (copy on file) and read into the record selected sections of the Estero Community Plan, including Objective 19.2 and Policy 19.2.1 (copy on file).
